summary: Epic Lovelight with Peter Green, solid Other One jam
blurb: The set is anchored by a monster Turn On Your Lovelight featuring guest Peter Green and Pigpen, followed by an expansive The Other One. China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider and Black Peter provide bright highlights, while High Time closes the set on a high note. Soundboard sources capture strong playing despite early mix quirks.
review:
On February 1, 1970 at New Orleans’ Warehouse the Dead delivered a benefit show that still feels fresh decades later. The night’s centerpiece is an unforgettable Turn On Your Lovelight – Jerry and Peter Green trade licks in a soaring duet while Pigpen adds his trademark harmonica grit and even a surprise rap cameo, stretching the jam past fourteen minutes. After a looser start (the band famously debates the beat on Beat It On Down the Line), they lock into a powerful The Other One that rolls out with the same kinetic energy. Highlights continue with a seamless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider segue and a gritty Black Peter, then a breezy High Time that rounds out the set. Both soundboard recordings capture the band’s fire; the Lee‑Smith SBD offers the clearest mix after the early balance hiccups, making it the go‑to source for this historic performance.

While Bob Weir sits front‑and‑center in the opening mix and the drums sit low at first, the overall musicianship is stellar. Phil’s bass finds its footing midway, underpinning Jerry’s twin‑tone guitar work that dominates the later numbers. Pigpen’s presence on Lovelight adds a unique flavor that only this era could produce. For Deadheads looking for an early‑70s gem with guest firepower and jam‑centric flow, this show is a must‑listen.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings are marked positive, repeatedly praising the epic Lovelight with Peter Green and Pigpen, the powerful Other One, and strong China Cat > Rider jam. No negative comments appear across analyses, indicating consistent high praise for performance.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard capture with the clearest mix after early balance issues; fully showcases Peter Green’s guest solo and the extended Lovelight.

key_highlights: Monster Turn On Your Lovelight featuring Peter Green and Pigpen's rap cameo
Extended, high‑energy The Other One jam
Seamless China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition
Strong performances of Black Peter and High Time despite early mix quirks

band_performance.Jerry: Shows powerful Fender Twin tone, trades licks with Peter Green during Lovelight and shines on the closing of Cold Rain And Snow.
band_performance.Phil: Bass sits low at the start but comes forward after the mid‑set mix shift, providing solid support throughout.
band_performance.Bob: Early mix pushes his guitar forward in the right channel, making him dominate the opening songs; his playing remains clear and energetic.
band_performance.Brent: Pigpen leads the epic Lovelight with harmonica grit and a surprise rap cameo, adding unique flavor to the jam.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums are low and occasionally inaudible early on but grow steadier after Bear's solo, contributing a tighter backbeat later.
band_performance.Billy: Shares the early drum balance issues; overall drumming improves in the second half of the set.
